metadata.name: beta0_normal_smile
metadata.description: Normal SABR (beta=0) smile across five strikes ITM/ATM/OTM. Beta=0 is a separate code branch (z = nu/alpha * (F-K), f_mid_beta=1, no log-moneyness correction) and must be guarded independently.
metadata.source_detail: Hagan et al. (2002) 'Managing Smile Risk', Wilmott Magazine, eq. 2.17b beta->0 limit and eq. 2.18c ATM. For beta=0: z = (nu/alpha)*(F-K); chi(z) = ln((sqrt(1-2*rho*z+z^2)+z-rho)/(1-rho)); factor1 = alpha (f_mid_beta=1, correction_term=1 since (1-beta)^2 terms vanish at beta=0); factor2 = z/chi(z); time_correction = (2-3*rho^2)*nu^2/24 (alpha and rho terms vanish at beta=0); vol = factor1*factor2*(1+T*time_correction). Derived independently in Python; NOT copied from SABRModel output. Parameters: alpha=0.006, beta=0.0, nu=0.30, rho=0.10, F=0.03, T=1.0. Manual derivation for K=0.02: z=(0.30/0.006)*(0.03-0.02)=0.5, chi(z)=0.492241014428602, factor1=0.006, factor2=1.015762574316171, time_correction=(2-3*0.01)/24*0.09=0.007387500, factor3=1.007387500, vol=0.006139599122004.

vol_k0010.tolerance_reason: Normal SABR closed-form; purely algebraic computation

vol_k0020.tolerance_reason: Normal SABR closed-form; manual derivation verified to 15 digits

vol_k0030.tolerance_reason: ATM case uses eq. 2.18c directly; alpha*(1+(2-3*rho^2)*nu^2/24*T)

vol_k0040.tolerance_reason: Normal SABR closed-form; purely algebraic computation

vol_k0050.tolerance_reason: Normal SABR closed-form; purely algebraic computation
